ac26_verdict_plumbing_note: Per AC-2.6 line-level rule: a finding is provably NEW when (i) the line falls within this cycle's diff hunks AND (ii) the finding is absent from the pre-diff baseline. Since the violations list is empty, no per-finding introduced_in_diff tag is required. If a violation had been emitted, it would be tagged introduced_in_diff: true|false per overlap + pre-diff-baseline check. The 5 raw CJK labels on line 333 fall WITHIN the diff hunk (new line) but are NOT flagged as a violation because they are the intentional content of the new Exemption declaration itself (paragraph-self-justifying, same epistemic class as the U+4E00 algorithm regex literal); the classify step of the Active CJK Detection Algorithm correctly suppresses them via the new Exemption clause.
